snp5.1:1.1:
From the delightful city of the Kosalans, a brahman [Bāvarī] who had mastered mantras,
aspiring to nothingness, went to the Southern country.
snp5.1:2.1: He, in the land of Assaka,
snp5.1:2.3: on the bank of the Godhāvarī,
snp5.1:3.2: Close by was a large village.
snp5.1:3.3: And with the income from that village
snp5.1:4.2: he returned again to his ashram.
snp5.1:4.3: As soon as he had entered there,
snp5.1:6.1: Bāvarī, on seeing him, invited him to sit down,
snp5.1:6.3: asked after what is pleasant & skillful,
snp5.1:7.1: “Whatever of mine that could be given away,
snp5.1:8.4: may your head split into seven pieces.”
snp5.1:10.1: He wasted away, taking no food,
snp5.1:10.2: afflicted with the arrow of grief,
snp5.1:10.3: and, with his mind this way,
snp5.1:10.4: his heart found no delight in jhāna.
snp5.1:12.1: “That imposter, wanting money, doesn’t discern heads;
snp5.1:12.4:
has no knowledge of heads or the splitting of heads.”
Bāvarī:
snp5.1:13.4:
I will listen to your words.”
The devatā:
snp5.1:14.4:
That is surely the insight of Victors.”
Bāvarī:
snp5.1:15.2: in this circle of the earth,
snp5.1:15.4:
Explain that to me, devatā.”
The devatā:
snp5.1:16.1: “Gone forth from the city of Kapilavatthu,
snp5.1:16.4: a Sakyan-son, a bringer of light:
snp5.1:17.2: gone to the far shore of all dhammas;
snp5.1:17.3: attainer of all direct knowledges & strengths,
snp5.1:17.4: one with an Eye that sees all dhammas;
snp5.1:17.5: attainer of the end of all action,
snp5.1:17.6: released in the ending of acquisitions.
snp5.1:18.1: He, the One with Eyes, awakened, blessed,
snp5.1:18.3: Going to him, you ask him.
snp5.1:20.4: or in which country is the protector of the world?
snp5.1:20.5: Going where can we pay homage
snp5.1:20.6:
to the Self-Awakened One, supreme among two-footed beings?”
The devatā:
snp5.1:21.1: “In Sāvatthī, the Kosalan city, is the Victor of vast discernment,
snp5.1:21.3: He, a matchless Sakyan-son, effluent-free, a bull among men,
snp5.1:21.4: is an expert in the splitting of heads.”
snp5.1:22.2: brahmans who had mastered the mantras,
snp5.1:23.1: He whose appearance in the world
snp5.1:23.3: has today arisen in the world,
snp5.1:23.6:
see the one supreme among two-footed beings.”
The students:
snp5.1:24.2: will we know for sure that he’s the One Self-Awakened?
snp5.1:24.4:
how we will know him.”
Bāvarī:
snp5.1:25.1: “There have come down in the mantras
snp5.1:25.2: the marks of a Great Man.
snp5.1:26.2: are the marks of a Great Man
snp5.1:27.1: If he dwells in a home,
snp5.1:27.4: but with righteousness— he will rule.
snp5.1:28.3: with his roof opened up, he will be Self-Awakened,
snp5.1:29.1: Ask just in your heart about my caste & clan,
snp5.1:29.3: and about heads & the splitting of heads.
snp5.1:30.4: the questions asked in your heart.”
snp5.1:34.3: endowed with jhāna, delighting in jhāna, enlightened,
snp5.1:34.4: perfume with perfumes from previous lives,
snp5.1:35.3: left, setting out for the North,
snp5.1:36.1: first to the establishment of Muḷaka,
snp5.1:38.2: to Vesālī, the city of the Magadhans,
snp5.1:38.3: and then to the Pāsāṇaka shrine,
snp5.1:39.1: Like a thirsty man for cool water,
snp5.1:39.2: like a merchant for a great profit,
snp5.1:39.3: like one burning from heat for shade,
snp5.1:40.1: And at that time, the Blessed One,
snp5.1:40.2: surrounded by the Saṅgha of monks,
snp5.1:40.3: was teaching the monks the Dhamma,
snp5.1:40.4: like a lion roaring in the forest.
snp5.1:41.2: like the sun with radiance in beams,
snp5.1:41.3: like the moon come to fullness
snp5.1:42.3: he stood to one side, overjoyed,
snp5.1:42.4: and in his heart asked the questions:
snp5.1:43.3: speak of his perfection in the mantras,
snp5.1:43.4:
and how many brahmans does he teach?”
The Buddha:
snp5.1:44.2: and by clan, he is a Bāvarī.
snp5.1:44.3: Three are the marks in his body,
snp5.1:45.4:
In his own doctrine he has reached perfection.”
Ajita:
snp5.1:46.4:
don’t leave us in doubt.”
The Buddha:
snp5.1:47.1: “He can hide his face with his tongue,
snp5.1:47.2: he has a tuft of hair between his brows,
snp5.1:47.3: his male organ is in a sheath:
snp5.1:48.4: with hands palm-to-palm over their hearts, thought:
snp5.1:49.3: asked those questions in his heart?
snp5.1:49.4:
To whom did he [the Buddha] reply?”
Ajita:
snp5.1:50.4:
Subdue our doubt, seer.”
The Buddha:
snp5.1:51.2: and the splitting of the head, knowledge
snp5.1:52.2: the young brahman, putting himself in order,
snp5.1:52.3: arranging his deer-skin over one shoulder,
snp5.1:52.4: fell with his head at the Blessed One’s feet:
snp5.1:53.1: “Master, One with Eyes, Bāvarī the brahman,
snp5.1:53.2: together with his students, dear sir—
snp5.1:53.3: exultant in mind, happy at heart—
snp5.1:54.1: “May he be happy, Bāvarī the brahman,
snp5.1:54.3: And may you, too, be happy, young brahman,
snp5.1:55.3: Ask, now that I’ve given leave,
snp5.1:55.4: whatever you wish in your heart.”
snp5.1:56.1: Given leave by the Self-Awakened One,
snp5.1:56.2: having sat to one side, hands palm-to-palm over his heart,
snp5.1:56.3: Ajita there addressed the first question
